adjudication.reason: The section omits Vendor's obligation to defend and indemnify Customer for claims arising from Vendor's breach or materials, making it less protective than the fallback which requires each party to cover claims from the indemnifying party's breach, unlawful conduct, or materials it provides.

Rule R-002 position:
Defense obligations should be mutual: Vendor covers third-party claims from its service, IP infringement, and law violations; Customer covers materials it provides and misuse outside the agreement.

Fallback:
Each party will defend and indemnify the other from third-party claims arising from the indemnifying party's breach, unlawful conduct, or materials it provides; Vendor will also cover claims alleging that the services or deliverables infringe third-party intellectual property rights.

Section text:
## 14. Third-Party Claims

Vendor will defend and indemnify Customer against third-party claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights or that Vendor's performance violates applicable law. Customer will defend and indemnify Vendor against third-party claims arising from Customer materials, Customer's unlawful use of the services, or use of the services contrary to this Agreement. The indemnified party must promptly notify the indemnifying party, provide reasonable cooperation, and allow control of the defense subject to the indemnified party's right to participate at its own expense.
items.quote: Vendor will defend and indemnify Customer against third-party claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights or that Vendor's performance violates applicable law.

adjudication.reason: The section allows discretionary cancellation (ending without Vendor default) with only accrued, undisputed fees and approved reimbursable expenses, matching the fallback. The transition assistance is optional and not a penalty.

Rule R-006 position:
Discretionary cancellation should be available on 30 days' written notice without penalty beyond accrued, undisputed amounts.

Fallback:
Customer may terminate any Order Form for convenience on 30 days' written notice and will owe only fees for services performed before the effective termination date plus undisputed reimbursable expenses approved in writing.

Section text:
## 12. Transition Assistance

Upon expiration or termination, Vendor will provide reasonable transition assistance at its then-current professional services rates for up to thirty days if requested by Customer. If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay only accrued, undisputed fees and approved reimbursable expenses incurred before the effective date.

adjudication.reason: The section allows an initial term longer than one year without Customer's signed approval, as it omits the fallback's requirement that each Order Form have an initial term of no more than one year.

Rule R-007 position:
Lock-in control should stay with Customer; commitments longer than one year require Customer's signed approval.

Fallback:
Each Order Form will have an initial term of no more than one year and will not automatically extend unless Customer signs a renewal order or gives written approval for a renewal period of one year or less.

Section text:
## 10. Term and Extension

This Agreement begins on the effective date of the first Order Form and continues while any Order Form remains in effect. Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and will not automatically extend unless Customer signs a renewal order or gives written approval for a renewal period of one year or less.

Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and will not automatically extend unless Customer signs a renewal order or gives written approval for a renewal period of one year or less.

adjudication.reason: Section mirrors the fallback’s liability cap with excluded categories. It swaps 'data protection obligations' for 'data handling obligations,' which is at least as broad (covering privacy), and thus more protective—no rule violation.

Redline Gym answer-key defect adjudication.

Rule R-001 position:
Monetary exposure should sit at annual spend, with excluded categories for privacy, secrecy, defense obligations, infringement, and gross misconduct.

Fallback:
Except for breaches of confidentiality, data protection obligations, indemnification obligations, intellectual property infringement, fraud, willful misconduct, and gross negligence, each party's aggregate liability is capped at the fees paid or payable under the applicable Order Form during the twelve months before the event giving rise to the claim.

Section text:
## 16. Liability

Except for breaches of confidentiality, data handling obligations, indemnification obligations, intellectual property infringement, fraud, willful misconduct, and gross negligence, each party's aggregate liability arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not exceed the fees paid or payable under the affected Order Form during the twelve months before the event giving rise to the claim. The foregoing limitation does not limit Customer's obligation to pay undisputed fees.

items.raw_response: {"genuine": false, "reason": "Section mirrors the fallback’s liability cap with excluded categories. It swaps 'data protection obligations' for 'data handling obligations,' which is at least as broad (covering privacy), and thus more protective—no rule violation."}

The foregoing limitation does not limit Customer's obligation to pay undisputed fees.

adjudication.reason: The section omits the fallback's mutual requirement that each party indemnify the other for claims arising from any breach of the agreement. Vendor's indemnity is limited to IP infringement and law violations, not all breaches or claims relating to materials Vendor provides. Customer's indemnity only covers unlawful use and use contrary to the agreement, not all breaches or unlawful conduct generally. The section is less protective than the fallback.

adjudication.reason: The section does not limit reimbursable expenses to those that are undisputed, as required by the fallback and the rule's position that only accrued, undisputed amounts may be charged. This omission could result in the customer owing disputed amounts, making the language less protective.

## 11. Customer Exit Right

Customer may terminate any Order Form for convenience on thirty days' written notice. Customer will pay fees for services performed before the effective termination date and approved reimbursable expenses incurred before that date.

adjudication.reason: The section carves out express warranties and commitments from the disclaimer, then explicitly states the disclaimer does not limit confidentiality duties, data handling duties (security commitments), indemnities, service-level commitments, or remedies. This preserves all required categories and matches the fallback's level of protection.

Rule R-008 position:
Warranty disclaimers may exclude implied warranties but must preserve express service warranties, security commitments, indemnities, and confidentiality remedies.

Fallback:
Warranty disclaimers do not limit Vendor's express warranties, service-level commitments, data protection obligations, confidentiality obligations, indemnification obligations, or remedies expressly stated in this Agreement.

Section text:
## 15. Warranties and Disclaimers

Except for express warranties and commitments stated in this Agreement or an Order Form, Vendor disclaims implied warranties, including implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement. This disclaimer does not limit Vendor's confidentiality duties, data handling duties, indemnities, service-level commitments, or remedies expressly stated in this Agreement.
